title: MutSpec: Finding Latent Bugs via Formally Grounded Mutation-Specification Duality
core_insight: The partition of a function's mutants into killed and survived sets implicitly defines a specification: killed mutants witness what tests enforce, survived mutants witness what tests permit. This duality is made constructive via WP differencing and lattice-walk synthesis.

program_model.language: Loop-free first-order imperative programs over QF-LIA

expressions.arithmetic: a ::= n | x | a + a | a - a | a * c | -a
expressions.relational: r ::= a < a | a <= a | a = a | a != a | a >= a | a > a
expressions.logical: b ::= r | b AND b | b OR b | NOT b | true | false

syntax.statements: S ::= x := e | if b then S else S | S; S | return e | assert b

program_model.semantics: Denotational: [[f]]: Z^n -> Z maps input tuples to integer outputs. For branching programs, [[f]](x) follows the unique execution path determined by x. No loops, no recursion, no exceptions beyond assertion failures.
program_model.fragment_scope: QF-LIA: all expressions are linear in program variables (no x*y, only c*x for constant c). Quantifier-free specifications only. Array access a[i] treated as uninterpreted function for index reasoning.

AOR.name: Arithmetic Operator Replacement
AOR.definition: Replace binary arithmetic op in {+,-,*,/,%} with each alternative
AOR.QF_LIA_restriction: Only +/- replacements stay within QF-LIA fragment
AOR.error_predicate: For + -> -: E(m)(x) = (b(x) != 0) where subexpr is a(x)+b(x)

ROR.name: Relational Operator Replacement
ROR.definition: Replace relational op in {<,<=,==,!=,>=,>} with each alternative

LCR.name: Logical Connector Replacement
LCR.error_predicate: For AND -> OR: E(m)(x) = (NOT p(x) AND q(x)) OR (p(x) AND NOT q(x))

UOI.name: Unary Operator Insertion
UOI.definition: Insert unary minus (a -> -a) or negation (b -> NOT b)

mutation_operators.total_mutant_count: O(n * k) where n = operator sites, k = bounded constant

specification_lattice.carrier: QF-LIA formulas over program variables and return value
specification_lattice.ordering: phi <= psi iff phi |= psi (phi implies psi, phi is stronger)
specification_lattice.key_property: Decidable ordering via SMT for QF-LIA

D1_error_predicate.name: Error Predicate
D1_error_predicate.statement: For function f and mutant m, E(m): X -> {true,false} is defined as E(m)(x) = (m(x) != f(x)). Characterizes inputs where mutant diverges.

D2_mutation_induced_spec.name: Mutation-Induced Specification
D2_mutation_induced_spec.statement: phi_M(f,T) = AND_{m in MKill} NOT E(m). Conjunction of negated error predicates for all killed mutants.

properties.completeness_wrt_killed: For every killed m: m |!= phi_M(f,T)
properties.maximality: phi_M(f,T) is weakest spec separating f from all killed mutants

D3_discrimination_lattice.name: Discrimination Lattice
D3_discrimination_lattice.statement: sigma: P(MKill) -> Spec sending S to AND_{m in S} NOT E(m) is a lattice homomorphism. Its image is a finite sub-lattice of Spec.

D4_mutation_adequacy.name: Mutation-Adequate Test Suite
D4_mutation_adequacy.statement: T is mutation-adequate for operator set M and function f iff T kills every killable mutant in M(f).

D5_gap_witness.name: Gap Witness
D5_gap_witness.statement: A triple (m, x0, c) where m is surviving non-equivalent, x0 is a distinguishing input, c is the violated contract clause. A formal bug report.
D5_gap_witness.drives_module: Gap Analysis Engine, Bug Report Generation

T1_epsilon_completeness.name: Epsilon-Completeness of Standard Mutation Operators for QF-LIA
T1_epsilon_completeness.statement: Let f be a loop-free program over QF-LIA and M = {AOR, ROR, LCR, UOI}. M is epsilon-complete for QF-LIA: for every non-tautological QF-LIA property phi with f |= phi, if phi can be violated by a single-site semantic change, then some m in M(f) witnesses it. If T is mutation-adequate, then phi_M(f,T) is the strongest QF-LIA specification satisfied by f.

proof_structure.approach: Case analysis over QF-LIA atomic predicates and loop-free program structure
steps: Step 1: Every QF-LIA atom has form sum_i(a_i * x_i) + c REL 0
Step 2: For each operator site, the relevant mutation class covers all boundary-crossing behaviors
Step 3: Coverage argument via partition of f's semantics into piecewise-linear regions
Step 4: Single-site sufficiency for atomic predicates
proof_structure.key_lemma: Operator Exhaustiveness: For each QF-LIA atomic predicate P over a single operator site s, there exists m in {AOR,ROR,LCR,UOI}(f) at s such that E(m) AND P is satisfiable.
limitations: Site-independence premise required for multi-conjunct properties
QF-LIA only (no nonlinear, string, heap properties)

T1_epsilon_completeness.consequence: Mutation-adequate tests provably determine strongest QF-LIA spec

T2_subsumption_implication.name: Subsumption-Implication Correspondence
T2_subsumption_implication.statement: m1 subsumes m2 iff E(m2) |= E(m1). Dominator set D yields phi_M(D) = phi_M(MKill) with |D| = O(n) vs |MKill| = O(nk).

T3_wp_composition_completeness.name: WP-Composition Completeness (Restricted Fragment)
T3_wp_composition_completeness.statement: For single-expression functions, Boolean closure of WP-differences contains every mutation-derivable QF-LIA contract. Lattice-walk = SyGuS power.
T3_wp_composition_completeness.positive_case: Single-expression: WP differences are QF-LIA atoms, Boolean closure generates full algebra
T3_wp_composition_completeness.negative_case: Multi-branch: cross-path relational properties (e.g., ret=max(a,b)) not in closure

T4_lattice_walk_properties.name: Lattice-Walk Termination, Soundness, and Irredundancy
T4_lattice_walk_properties.statement: The lattice-walk terminates in O(|D|^2 * SMT(n)) time producing a contract that is sound, complete w.r.t. killed mutants, and irredundant.

proof_sketch.termination: Finite lattice, strictly descending chain, bounded by |MKill|
proof_sketch.soundness: Each clause derived from f's WP, so f satisfies conjunction
proof_sketch.completeness: Each killed mutant's error predicate negated in some clause
proof_sketch.irredundancy: Explicit entailment check removes redundant conjuncts

gap_theorem.name: Gap Theorem
gap_theorem.statement: Every surviving mutant is either equivalent or non-equivalent. Non-equivalent survivors violating the inferred contract are formal bug witnesses with concrete distinguishing inputs.
gap_theorem.consequence: Transforms mutation analysis into formally grounded bug detection

A1_wp_differencing.name: WP Differencing for Error Predicate Extraction
A1_wp_differencing.complexity: O(n^2) total with batch sharing, O(1) SMT queries per mutant

A2_lattice_walk.name: Lattice-Walk Contract Synthesis
steps: For each dominator d_i: if phi does not exclude d_i, strengthen phi := phi AND NOT E(d_i)
Irredundancy pass: remove conjuncts implied by others

A3_gap_analysis.name: Gap Analysis for Bug Detection
steps: For each surviving m: check equivalence via Z3

complexity_analysis.per_function: O(n^2 * SMT(n)) dominated by lattice walk and WP differencing
complexity_analysis.scalability: 10K-function codebase in overnight CI window (8-12 hours)
complexity_analysis.bottleneck: SMT solving time, mitigated by incremental Z3 and per-site batching

assumptions.assumption: PIT descriptors sufficient for source-level replay (>=80%)

wp_engine_hard.mitigation: Budget 8K LoC, scope to scalar first
